data.title: Update test-ticket-lifecycle.sh: assert .archived marker is written after ARCHIVED event commit
data.description:
## Testing Mode
UPDATE

## What
Modify tests/scripts/test-ticket-lifecycle.sh to add a test asserting that ticket-lifecycle.sh writes a .archived marker file into each ticket's directory after writing the ARCHIVED event and committing.

## Test to add (must FAIL before T6 implementation)

test_lifecycle_writes_archived_marker:
  Given: a temp git tracker repo containing a closed, archive-eligible ticket (needs a CREATE event, STATUS=closed event, and no existing ARCHIVED event)
  When: ticket-lifecycle.sh runs against the temp tracker
  Then: <tracker_dir>/<ticket_id>/.archived marker file exists after the script completes
  AND: The .archived marker was written AFTER the ARCHIVED event file (verify by checking mtime ordering: ARCHIVED event file mtime <= .archived marker mtime)
  AND: The original ARCHIVED event file also exists in the ticket dir

## Files to modify
- tests/scripts/test-ticket-lifecycle.sh (MODIFY — add test function)

## Note on write ordering
The mtime check (ARCHIVED event file written before or equal to marker) verifies SC 2's write-order requirement — marker is written AFTER the event is durable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New test function exists in test-ticket-lifecycle.sh
  Verify: grep -q "test_lifecycle_writes_archived_marker\|archived_marker" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-ticket-lifecycle.sh
- [ ] Test FAILS before implementation (lifecycle script doesn't write marker yet)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-ticket-lifecycle.sh test_lifecycle_writes_archived_marker 2>/dev/null; test $? -ne 0
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh exits 0 (existing lifecycle tests still pass; new failing test gated by RED marker in .test-index)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && bash tests/run-all.sh
